2603.17087.title: Ensemble Self-Training for Unsupervised Machine Translation
2603.17087.paper_summary: We present an ensemble-driven self-training framework for unsupervised neural machine translation (UNMT). Starting from a primary language pair, we train multiple UNMT models that share the same translation task but differ in an auxiliary language, inducing structured diversity across models. We then generate pseudo-translations for the primary pair using token-level ensemble decoding, averaging model predictions in both directions. These ensemble outputs are used as synthetic parallel data to further train each model, allowing the models to improve via shared supervision. At deployment time, we select a single model by validation performance, preserving single-model inference cost. Experiments show statistically significant improvements over single-model UNMT baselines, with mean gains of 1.7 chrF when translating from English and 0.67 chrF when translating into English.
2603.17087.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個基於集成的自我訓練框架，用於無監督神經機器翻譯（UNMT）。從一對主要語言開始，我們訓練多個UNMT模型，這些模型共享相同的翻譯任務，但在輔助語言上有所不同，從而在模型之間引入結構化的多樣性。接著，我們使用詞元級集成解碼生成主要語言對的偽翻譯，對模型預測進行雙向平均。這些集成輸出被用作合成平行數據，以進一步訓練每個模型，讓模型通過共享監督來改進。在部署時，我們根據驗證性能選擇一個單一模型，保持單模型推斷成本。實驗顯示，相較於單模型UNMT基準，統計上顯著提高，從英語翻譯的平均增益為1.7 chrF，翻譯成英語的平均增益為0.67 chrF。
2603.17087.author: Ido Aharon et.al.
2603.17087.authors: Ido Aharon, Jonathan Shaki, Sarit Kraus
